sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the orange/blue blocks have moved DOWN and are now right next to the white cross. The orange block is to the right of the cross, and the blue block is below the orange block. The white cross is still visible.
Let me check the current positions to see if the player is now overlapping with the target or if I need to move LEFT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions after DOWN
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = {n['id']: n for n in seg['nodes']}

print("=== Player and target positions ===")
for nid, n in sorted(nodes.items()):
    if n['color'] in ['O', 'b', 'W', 'w']:
        print(f"  id={nid} color={n['color']} boundary={n['boundary']} pixels={n['pixels']}")

print(f"\nResult: {last_action_result}")

# Check if the player overlapped with the white cross
# Orange was at rows 25-26, now should be at rows 30-31
# Blue was at rows 27-29, now should be at rows 32-34
# White cross is at rows 31-33, cols 20-22

# The player moved DOWN 5 rows
# Orange: was row 25-26, now row 30-31
# Blue: was row 27-29, now row 32-34
# Target: row 31-33, col 20-22
# Player col: 24-28
# Need to move LEFT to align columns

# Try LEFT to move player onto the white cross
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
